texts.full_text: valid id? I am using following jquery selector by $("#rs_0.01").val(); So my small question is: this valid or not ? As it's not working in my case. A: Check documentation of jQuery Selectors To use any of the meta-characters as selectors, you need to escape them using a backslash (\). For example, - To select an element with id "div#myid", do: $("#div\#myid"). In your case: $("#rs_0.01") is not valid as it seems like the value of 'ID' attribute contains numbers which are in CSS selectors. Valid IDs can contain only letters (a-z), digits( 1-9 ), hyphens(minus sign) and underscores

texts.full_text: nine songs "Turn" was written by Charly Norman, with two others being co-written by him with Larry, and Charly's band, then called Softcore, providing the musical backing. Intended to be a pre-release to Behind the Curtain, the as yet unreleased first album in Norman's Second Trilogy, first mentioned in 1983, Tourniquet was described by Dougie Adam as "perhaps Larry's deepest, most articulate album ever ... [and] even more hard hitting than 'Only Visiting This Planet' or 'Stranded in Babylon'". texts.preview_tail: d Photo Information Copyright: Emile Gregoire (gregoire) (86) Genre: Animals Medium: Color Date Taken: 2005-08-10 Categories: Mammals Camera: Canon 350D / Digital Rebel XT, Canon EF75-300mm f/4-5.6 IS
texts.full_text: the hippos was awe-inspiring, a reminder of the raw power of the natural world. Day 3: We woke up to the sound of birdsong, a symphony that was a world away from the city noises we were used to. Today, we were to explore the Chobe River, a lifeline that sustains the wildlife of the Chobe National Park. Our first sighting was a group of impalas, their sleek bodies a picture of grace. But the real treat was yet to come. As we rounded a bend, we were greeted by the sight of a pride of lions, their golden coats a stark contrast to the lush green of the savannah. The lions were a sight to behold, their majesty a stark reminder of the wild, untamed beauty of the African savannah. Day 4: Our last day in Botswana was a bittersweet one. We spent the day in the Chobe National Park, a place of stark beauty. The park was a tapestry of colors, the green of the savannah, the blue of the Chobe River, the golden hues of the setting sun. We spotted a herd of buffaloes, their hulking forms a stark contrast to the delicate beauty of the impalas. As the sun set, we toasted to the end of a memorable journey, a journey of discovery, of beauty, of the raw, untamed power of the African wild. Our journey through Botswana was more than just a safari, it was a journey of discovery, of beauty, of the raw, untamed power of the African wild. It was a journey of a lifetime. Next, we head to the Victoria Falls, a journey of a different kind, a journey of power, of beauty, of the raw, untamed power of the African wild. But that, as they say, is a story for another time. << Previous Next >> Fooling around Photo Information Copyright: Emile Gregoire (gregoire) (86) Genre: Animals Medium: Color Date Taken: 2005-08-10 Categories: Mammals Camera: Canon 350D / Digital Rebel XT, Canon EF75-300mm f/4-5.6 IS

texts.full_text: d after the court granted the motion of defendant, James Hohenshelt, doing business as JMK Enterprises *1600 (JMK) for summary judgment.[1] The court determined that the lease agreement which JMK entered into with defendant Christopher Allen was a sallease which, under the doctrine of merger, was merged with the underlying lease agreement between JMK and the defendant, Christopher Allen, which was entered into prior to the lease agreement between JMK and the Plaintiff, Diana Medrano. The Plaintiff, Diana Medrano, appeals from the judgment entered after the court granted the motion of defendant, James Hohenshelt, doing business as JMK Enterprises, for summary judgment. The court determined that the lease agreement which JMK entered into with defendant Christopher Allen was a conditional sale lease agreement, which, under the doctrine of merger, was merged with the underlying lease agreement between JMK and the defendant, Christopher Allen, which was entered into prior to the lease agreement between JMK and the Plaintiff, Diana Medrano. The Plaintiff, Diana Medrano, appeals from the judgment entered after the court granted the motion of defendant, James Hohenshelt, doing business as JMK Enterprises, for summary judgment. The court determined that the lease agreement which JMK entered into with defendant Christopher Allen was a conditional sale lease agreement, which, under the doctrine of merger, was merged with the underlying lease agreement between JMK and the defendant, Christopher Allen, which was entered into prior to the lease agreement between JMK
